_README: This file controls how studies move through their lifecycle stages.
The system re-reads this file every 30 seconds.
Changes affect NEWLY CREATED studies only (existing studies keep their pre-determined timelines).
Each transition has a min and max delay in SECONDS.
When a study is created, the system randomly picks a delay within each range
and pre-computes the ENTIRE timeline upfront.
Example: if Introduced_to_Assigned has min=60, max=300,
the system picks a random number between 60 and 300 seconds.
If it picks 150, the study will be assigned exactly 150 seconds (2.5 minutes) after being introduced.
cancellation_probability: chance (0.0 to 1.0) that a newly created study will be
pre-marked for cancellation at a random stage instead of going to Approved.

Introduced_to_Assigned._comment: 1 to 5 minutes after appearing in worklist, it gets assigned to a radiologist.

Assigned_to_Reading._comment: 30 seconds to 2 minutes after assignment, the radiologist starts reading.

Reading_to_Pending_Approval._comment: 2 to 15 minutes of reading time before submitting for approval.

Pending_Approval_to_Approved._comment: 1 to 10 minutes in the approval queue before final approval.
